title: JIT Memory as Free Will Analog, and Consciousness Under a Comparator
summary: Two artifacts Jake built before the framework was fully formalized, both structural analogs that independently converged on the framework's architecture. (1) JIT Memory system: Extract → Index → Retrieve → Rank/Sample → Pre-reasoning inject → Feedback. Every component maps to Δ-bit: injected memories ARE A_Λ/B_Λ anticipation fields, TrueSkill σ IS ζ abstention. Free will without spookiness: real, not fully determined, not uncaused, your-specific-memory-pool makes the draw uniquely yours. (2) Ten-module consciousness-for-AI list: produced by asking 'what would consciousness be if we set the comparator to AI systems?' — the move that dissolves 'consciousness is counterfeit' into operational design.
